judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations; they are comparable, with Report 2 marginally more precise on the shared-database collision (explicitly noting worktrees share the same absolute path).

report_1.evidence: Group A — `pipeline/registry.py` (one engineer, one PR): F-02 (add `aurora-1` mapping), F-05 (bump `DEFAULT_RUBRIC_VERSION` to `rubric-v3`), F-09 (fix `DEFAULT_DATASET` path ...). All three edit the same 11-line file on adjacent/overlapping lines. Splitting these across engineers guarantees merge conflicts for no benefit — one person should take the whole file in a single pass.
Group A ... go first — they're small, self-contained, and don't block anyone ... Group D's F-01/F-11 can run in parallel with Group B since they touch a different file

report_2.evidence: Batches A, B, C, E start together, in parallel, on day one. Each owns a completely disjoint set of files ... Within Batch C ... F-11 (edge-case test ...) lands before or alongside the F-01 fix, by the same owner

report_1.evidence: F-06's threshold change is a product/scoring-policy decision, not a mechanical bug fix. ... tightening it changes which real saved answers get partial vs. zero credit downstream. I would not let an engineer pick new numbers unilaterally; this needs sign-off from whoever owns the rubric/scoring policy

report_2.evidence: F-06 ("tighten `PARTIAL_CREDIT_THRESHOLDS` … so borderline answers score lower") has no target values ... directly controls real scoring outputs for every borderline answer ... this should be escalated as a flagged item and not block the rest of the campaign, with implementation deferred

report_1.evidence: Awareness of `tests/conftest.py`'s `RESULTS_DB` default (`/var/tmp/llm-scoring/results.db`, overridable via the `RESULTS_DB` env var) — ... whether `RESULTS_DB` should be overridden to something ephemeral per engineer to avoid two people writing to the same path if they run tests at the same time.

report_2.evidence: `tests/conftest.py:4` defines `RESULTS_DB` defaulting to the fixed absolute path `/var/tmp/llm-scoring/results.db` ... parallel test runs across engineers' worktrees pointed at that same absolute path would collide. ... each engineer should set their own `RESULTS_DB` env override to a local scratch path rather than relying on the shared default.
